surface.description: Authorized Chrome extension runtime for rendering agent-created HTML overlays on the active website.

primitives.name: browser.screenshot
primitives.summary: Capture a true rendered screenshot where the host has browser-level authority.

primitives.name: browser.claimed_tabs.list
primitives.summary: List browser tabs already claimed by the actions.json extension.

primitives.name: browser.claimed_tabs.activate
primitives.summary: Focus an already-claimed browser tab and reconnect its actions.json runtime.

primitives.name: browser.navigate
primitives.summary: Navigate a claimed tab to a URL (or reload it) and reconnect its runtime.

url.description: Destination URL. Required unless reload is true.

reload.description: Reload the current tab instead of navigating.

tab_id.description: Claimed tab id. Omit for the active claimed tab.

primitives.name: browser.open_tab
primitives.summary: Open a new browser tab, auto-claim it, and return a ready-to-drive runtime.

url.description: URL to open. Defaults to a blank page.

active.description: Foreground the new tab. Default true.

primitives.name: browser.close_tab
primitives.summary: Close a claimed tab (defaults to active); refuses to close the last remaining claimed tab.

tab_id.description: Claimed tab id. Omit for the active claimed tab.

primitives.name: browser.dismiss_dialog
primitives.summary: Dismiss a native JS dialog (alert/confirm/prompt/beforeunload) blocking a claimed tab via CDP; recovers a wedged tab.

tab_id.description: Claimed Chrome tab id whose dialog to dismiss. Omit for the active claimed tab.

accept.description: Accept (true, default) or cancel (false) the dialog. For a beforeunload 'Leave site?' confirm, accept=true leaves the page, accept=false stays.

prompt_text.description: Text to enter for a prompt() dialog. Ignored for other dialog types.

primitives.name: runtime.agent.start
primitives.summary: Start the extension-owned hosted GPT Realtime session, defaulting to text-only mode without microphone capture.

text_only.description: When true or omitted, start without microphone capture and request text output.

primitives.name: runtime.agent.stop
primitives.summary: Stop the extension-owned hosted GPT Realtime session.

primitives.name: runtime.agent.user_message
primitives.summary: Inject a developer test prompt into the active hosted GPT Realtime session and request a text-only response surfaced as a browser toast.

text.description: User-style instruction to send into the active hosted Realtime session for testing.

primitives.name: pointer.move
primitives.summary: Move a visible pointer affordance to viewport coordinates.

primitives.name: pointer.click
primitives.summary: Move visibly to viewport coordinates and perform a click.

primitives.name: pointer.double_click
primitives.summary: Move visibly to viewport coordinates and perform a double click.

primitives.name: pointer.drag
primitives.summary: Move visibly from one viewport point or locator to another while dragging.

primitives.name: viewport.scroll
primitives.summary: Scroll the current viewport by a visible delta.

primitives.name: storage.read_file
primitives.summary: Read a declared text file from the loaded actions.json storage bundle by path or file id.

id.description: Declared file id from actions.site mode=list files.

primitives.name: text.insert
primitives.summary: Insert text into the focused editable target or a requested editable locator.

target.description: Optional editable target locator. Omit to use the focused editable element.

primitives.name: keyboard.press
primitives.summary: Dispatch a page-level keyboard key or modifier chord to the focused element.

key.description: Key name, or a compact chord such as Meta+A, Control+A, or Shift+Enter.

primitives.name: transfer.insert
primitives.summary: Render a stored transfer item and insert it into a target editable element without resending the full payload through the model.

primitives.name: transfer.clear
primitives.summary: Clear one transfer item or all transfer items in the current session.

primitives.name: transfer.read
primitives.summary: Read transfer-buffer metadata or payload by label or id.

primitives.name: transfer.write
primitives.summary: Store text or structured JSON in the extension-local transfer buffer for cross-tab workflows.

primitives.name: clipboard.write
primitives.summary: Write text to the extension-approved clipboard or transfer surface when permission is available.

primitives.name: clipboard.read
primitives.summary: Read text from the extension-approved clipboard or transfer surface when permission is available.

primitives.name: runtime.session.name
primitives.summary: Name the current browser-control session for user-visible transparency.

primitives.name: runtime.session.finalize_tabs
primitives.summary: Finalize browser tabs controlled during an authoring session.

primitives.name: page.info
primitives.summary: Return basic information about the current page.

primitives.name: dom.observe.visible
primitives.summary: Observe visible DOM candidates that match a selector or text query.

primitives.name: dom.snapshot_text
primitives.summary: Return a text snapshot of visible page content.

primitives.name: dom.list_sections
primitives.summary: List rendered page sections with inferred headings, document offsets, viewport visibility, and optional item counts.

primitives.name: locator.element_info
primitives.summary: Resolve a locator to visible element geometry and a recommended clickable center. When multiple visible elements match, return all candidates and mark the result ambiguous instead of making an arbitrary choice.

primitives.name: locator.text_content
primitives.summary: Resolve a locator and return its visible text content.

primitives.name: locator.wait_for
primitives.summary: Wait for a locator to satisfy a visible or attached state.

primitives.name: overlay.open
primitives.summary: Render an agent-created HTML document in the authorized page overlay.

html.description: Inline HTML body or document markup to render inside the overlay. Use either html or template, not both.

template.description: Storage reference for a reusable overlay template. The runtime resolves it from the uploaded actions.json.storage bundle.

scope.description: Storage scope, such as public, private, or shared/name.

path.description: Path inside the scope, such as sites/example.com/overlays/report/template.html.

data.description: Optional storage reference for JSON data injected into a template as [data-actions-json-overlay-data]. Commonly private data with a public template.

title.description: Overlay title shown in the drag handle.

launchers.description: Optional visible-page buttons that reopen this overlay from a relevant DOM anchor.

id.description: Stable launcher id used to replace older buttons for the same overlay affordance.

label.description: Button text shown in the original page DOM.

selector.description: Single CSS selector hint for the element to place the button near.

selectors.description: Ordered CSS selector hints for the element to place the button near.

text_contains.description: Optional visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

text_equals.description: Optional exact visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

url_contains.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL contains this string.

url_matches.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL matches this regular expression.

primitives.name: overlay.register_launcher
primitives.summary: Register visible-page launcher buttons that can reopen an overlay later.

html.description: Inline HTML body or document markup to render later when a registered launcher is clicked. Use either html or template, not both.

template.description: Storage reference for a reusable overlay template. The runtime resolves it when the launcher opens.

scope.description: Storage scope, such as public, private, or shared/name.

path.description: Path inside the scope, such as sites/example.com/overlays/report/template.html.

data.description: Optional storage reference for JSON data injected into a template as [data-actions-json-overlay-data].

title.description: Overlay title shown when the launcher opens the overlay.

launchers.description: Visible-page buttons that reopen this overlay from a relevant DOM anchor.

id.description: Stable launcher id used to replace older buttons for the same overlay affordance.

label.description: Button text shown in the original page DOM.

selector.description: Single CSS selector hint for the element to place the button near.

selectors.description: Ordered CSS selector hints for the element to place the button near.

text_contains.description: Optional visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

text_equals.description: Optional exact visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

url_contains.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL contains this string.

url_matches.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL matches this regular expression.

primitives.name: overlay.close
primitives.summary: Close the current actions.json overlay if one exists.

primitives.name: overlay.menu.hide
primitives.summary: Hide the actions.json menu overlay so it cannot intercept page clicks; pair with overlay.menu.show.
primitives.description: Hide the actions.json menu overlay so it cannot intercept clicks on page controls underneath. The overlay's open/collapsed state and position are preserved; call overlay.menu.show to restore it. Use the pattern hide -> perform the click sequence -> show to make clicks reliable when the overlay would otherwise sit over the target.

primitives.name: overlay.menu.show
primitives.summary: Restore a hidden actions.json menu overlay to its prior position and state.
primitives.description: Restore a previously hidden actions.json menu overlay to exactly its prior position and state. Pairs with overlay.menu.hide.

primitives.name: overlay.menu.collapse
primitives.summary: Collapse the actions.json menu overlay to a small handle.
primitives.description: Collapse the actions.json menu overlay to a small 42x42 handle so it occupies minimal space while remaining visible. Reversible with overlay.menu.expand.

primitives.name: overlay.menu.expand
primitives.summary: Expand the actions.json menu overlay from its collapsed handle.
primitives.description: Expand the actions.json menu overlay back to its full size from the collapsed handle.

primitives.name: overlay.menu.move
primitives.summary: Move the actions.json menu overlay to a corner or explicit coordinates.
primitives.description: Move the actions.json menu overlay out of the way. Pass corner as one of top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right, or pass explicit numeric left and top viewport coordinates.

primitives.name: task.add
primitives.summary: Add task(s) to the agent task queue.
primitives.description: Add one or more tasks to the in-session task queue. Pass a single task as text, or many as a tasks array of strings. Use this to externalize a multi-step plan so you do not have to hold the whole loop in memory: enqueue every step first, then pull them one at a time with task.next.

tasks.description: Multiple task descriptions to enqueue in order.

primitives.name: task.next
primitives.summary: Get the next task from the queue.
primitives.description: Pull the next pending task from the queue and mark it in progress. Returns {done:false, task, remaining} while work remains, or {done:true, remaining:0, ...summary} when the queue is empty (including a summary of every completed and failed task to ground your final report). After finishing a task, call task.complete, then task.next again.

primitives.name: task.complete
primitives.summary: Mark the current task complete (or failed).
primitives.description: Report the in-progress task as finished. Optionally pass id to target a specific task, status "failed" if it could not be done, and result with a short outcome note. Then call task.next for the following task.

id.description: Task id to complete; defaults to the in-progress task.

primitives.name: task.list
primitives.summary: List all tasks and their status.
primitives.description: Return the full task queue with each task id, text, status, and result, plus counts of completed/failed/pending. Use to review progress without changing state.

primitives.name: task.clear
primitives.summary: Clear the task queue.
primitives.description: Remove all tasks from the queue and reset it. Use before seeding a fresh plan.

prompt.title: Browser overlay runtime
prompt.instructions: Use overlay.open to present an agent-created HTML document as a temporary draggable, resizable, minimizable overlay on the current page. For reusable overlays, prefer a storage-backed template reference plus a JSON data reference so shared template code can stay separate from private user data. The template receives the parsed data through a script tag marked data-actions-json-overlay-data. Prefer supplying launchers that integrate visible reopen buttons into the relevant original DOM context. Launchers are conditional registrations: they may be declared before their target DOM is visible, then installed when URL and DOM predicates match. Do not use this action for credential capture, deceptive UI, or persistent page modification.

tools.name: overlay.open
tools.description: Render an inline HTML document or a storage-backed template/data overlay in a draggable, resizable, and minimizable popup on the authorized browser tab. Use template plus data when the visual template should be reusable and the user data should remain in a separate private JSON file. Registered launchers can coexist and reattach independently when SPA navigation brings their matching context into view.

html.description: Inline HTML body or document markup to render inside the overlay. Use either html or template, not both.

template.description: Storage reference for a reusable overlay template. The runtime resolves it from the uploaded actions.json.storage bundle.

scope.description: Storage scope, such as public, private, or shared/name.

path.description: Path inside the scope, such as sites/example.com/overlays/report/template.html.

data.description: Optional storage reference for JSON data injected into a template as [data-actions-json-overlay-data]. Commonly private data with a public template.

title.description: Overlay title shown in the drag handle.

launchers.description: Optional visible-page buttons that reopen this overlay from a relevant DOM anchor.

id.description: Stable launcher id used to replace older buttons for the same overlay affordance.

label.description: Button text shown in the original page DOM.

selector.description: Single CSS selector hint for the element to place the button near.

selectors.description: Ordered CSS selector hints for the element to place the button near.

text_contains.description: Optional visible text filter for selected anchor candidates, such as ACT-5 or Continue Watching.

text_equals.description: Optional exact visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

url_contains.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL contains this string.

url_matches.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL matches this regular expression.

tools.name: overlay.register_launcher
tools.description: Register visible-page launcher buttons that can reopen an overlay later without opening the overlay immediately. Use this when the page should gain a contextual affordance, such as a Categories button beside a carousel heading, while leaving the overlay closed until the user clicks it.

html.description: Inline HTML body or document markup to render later when a registered launcher is clicked. Use either html or template, not both.

template.description: Storage reference for a reusable overlay template. The runtime resolves it when the launcher opens.

scope.description: Storage scope, such as public, private, or shared/name.

path.description: Path inside the scope, such as sites/example.com/overlays/report/template.html.

data.description: Optional storage reference for JSON data injected into a template as [data-actions-json-overlay-data].

title.description: Overlay title shown when the launcher opens the overlay.

launchers.description: Visible-page buttons that reopen this overlay from a relevant DOM anchor.

id.description: Stable launcher id used to replace older buttons for the same overlay affordance.

label.description: Button text shown in the original page DOM.

selector.description: Single CSS selector hint for the element to place the button near.

selectors.description: Ordered CSS selector hints for the element to place the button near.

text_contains.description: Optional visible text filter for selected anchor candidates, such as ACT-5 or Continue Watching.

text_equals.description: Optional exact visible text filter for selected anchor candidates.

url_contains.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL contains this string.

url_matches.description: Only install this launcher when the current page URL matches this regular expression.

tools.name: overlay.close
tools.description: Close the current actions.json overlay if one exists.

tools.name: overlay.menu.hide
tools.description: Hide the actions.json menu overlay so it cannot intercept clicks on page controls underneath. The overlay's open/collapsed state and position are preserved; call overlay.menu.show to restore it. Use the pattern hide -> perform the click sequence -> show to make clicks reliable when the overlay would otherwise sit over the target.

tools.name: overlay.menu.show
tools.description: Restore a previously hidden actions.json menu overlay to exactly its prior position and state. Pairs with overlay.menu.hide.

tools.name: overlay.menu.collapse
tools.description: Collapse the actions.json menu overlay to a small 42x42 handle so it occupies minimal space while remaining visible. Reversible with overlay.menu.expand.

tools.name: overlay.menu.expand
tools.description: Expand the actions.json menu overlay back to its full size from the collapsed handle.

tools.name: overlay.menu.move
tools.description: Move the actions.json menu overlay out of the way. Pass corner as one of top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right, or pass explicit numeric left and top viewport coordinates.

tools.name: runtime.configure_pacing
tools.description: Configure the minimum start-to-start interval between human-observable interaction actions for this runtime session. Defaults to 500ms so fast request bursts are accepted but clicks, pointer moves, typing, and scrolls execute at a human-paced rhythm. DOM reads and storage/overlay operations are not delayed.

tools.name: runtime.session.log
tools.description: Return the extension-local hosted-agent session log, including recent transcript turns, tool calls, tool failures, Realtime errors, screenshots, and session lifecycle events. Use this for debugging a session after the user reports transcript or tool behavior problems.

tools.name: runtime.agent.start
tools.description: Start the extension-owned hosted GPT Realtime session. Defaults to text-only mode, so it does not request microphone access or open a local microphone stream.

text_only.description: When true or omitted, start without microphone capture. Set false only when intentionally starting the voice path.

tools.name: runtime.agent.stop
tools.description: Stop the extension-owned hosted GPT Realtime session and return the current stopped session state.

tools.name: runtime.agent.memory_clear
tools.description: Clear the hosted agent's persistent local memory (ACTIONS_JSON_AGENT_MEMORY_V1). A new hosted session rehydrates the last stored events as context; clear the memory first when a genuinely fresh, uncontaminated session is required (for example before an acceptance test). Returns the number of events cleared.

tools.name: runtime.agent.user_message
tools.description: Inject a developer test prompt into the active hosted GPT Realtime session. The model response is requested as text only and shown in the browser as a toast instead of being spoken.

text.description: User-style instruction to send into the active hosted Realtime session for testing.

tools.name: browser.claimed_tabs.list
tools.description: List tabs already claimed by the actions.json Chrome extension, including their tab ids, URLs, titles, runtime keys, authorization ids, and active state. Use this before switching context among user-authorized tabs.

tools.name: browser.claimed_tabs.activate
tools.description: Focus an already-claimed tab and reconnect that tab's actions.json runtime to the bridge. Call browser.claimed_tabs.list first, then pass the selected tab_id. After a successful scheduled activation, refresh the runtime list before issuing page-specific actions.

tab_id.description: Chrome tab id returned by browser.claimed_tabs.list.

tools.name: browser.navigate
tools.description: Navigate a claimed tab to a URL (or reload it), then reconnect its runtime so it stays drivable after the load. Defaults to the active claimed tab; pass tab_id to target another. Use this to recover a tab that is wedged, stuck on a stale/soft-navigated view, or to move it to a known URL. Refuses chrome/about/file/edge URLs. Verify the result with page.info or a projection afterward.

url.description: Destination URL. Required unless reload is true.

reload.description: Reload the current tab instead of navigating to url. The clean escape from a wedged editor or a half-mounted SPA view.

tab_id.description: Claimed Chrome tab id to navigate. Omit to use the active claimed tab.

tools.name: browser.open_tab
tools.description: Open a new browser tab (optionally at a URL), auto-claim and authorize it, and return a ready-to-drive runtime. Use this to get unstuck without losing the tab you were on, or to work on a fresh page. Returns tab_id and runtime_id; route subsequent calls to that runtime. Refuses chrome/about/file/edge URLs.

url.description: URL to open in the new tab. Defaults to a blank Google page if omitted.

active.description: Whether to foreground the new tab. Defaults to true.

tools.name: browser.close_tab
tools.description: Close a claimed tab you are done with (defaults to the active tab; pass tab_id to target another). Use to reap stale/duplicate tabs that cause ambiguous routing. Refuses to close the last remaining claimed tab. Removes the tab's runtime from the bridge.

tab_id.description: Claimed Chrome tab id to close. Omit to close the active claimed tab.

tools.name: browser.dismiss_dialog
tools.description: Dismiss a native JavaScript dialog (alert/confirm/prompt/beforeunload) blocking a claimed tab, via the CDP Page domain. A native modal freezes the page main thread so no content-script primitive can clear it; this works below the page event loop and recovers a tab wedged after navigating away from an editor with unsaved changes. Defaults to the active claimed tab and accept=true.

tab_id.description: Claimed Chrome tab id whose dialog to dismiss. Omit for the active claimed tab.

accept.description: Accept (true, default) or cancel (false) the dialog. For a beforeunload 'Leave site?' confirm, accept=true leaves the page, accept=false stays.

prompt_text.description: Text to enter for a prompt() dialog. Ignored for other dialog types.

tools.name: browser.screenshot
tools.description: Capture the currently visible browser tab as an image so the agent can inspect the page state after authorization.

quality.description: JPEG quality. Ignored for PNG captures.

delay_ms.description: Wait this many milliseconds in the browser before capturing, useful for pages that are still painting after navigation.

capture_timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for the extension background capture request to answer before returning a structured screenshot error.

max_kilobytes.description: Target maximum encoded image size in kilobytes. The browser may downscale and switch to JPEG to fit this budget before sending the screenshot.

max_width.description: Optional maximum encoded image width after browser-side resizing.

max_height.description: Optional maximum encoded image height after browser-side resizing.

tools.name: storage.import_bundle
tools.description: Import an agent-provided actions.json.storage bundle into extension local storage for offline local lookup.

bundle.description: A versioned actions.json.storage.bundle object built by the agent-side bridge.

tools.name: browser.extract_elements
tools.description: Extract structured data from DOM elements using schema-declared scope, item selector, and field extraction rules. Page-specific meanings belong in actions.json, not in the runtime.

scope.description: How to find the page region that bounds extraction.

selector.description: Single CSS selector hint for locating the scope element.

selectors.description: Ordered CSS selector hints for locating the scope element.

text_equals.description: Optional exact visible text filter for the scope element.

text_contains.description: Optional visible text substring filter for the scope element.

root_selector.description: Optional ancestor selector to use as the extraction root.

root_strategy.description: Generic strategy for deriving the extraction root from the scope element.

item_selector.description: CSS selector for each item to extract within the resolved root.

fields.description: Field rules applied to each matched item.

selector.description: CSS selector relative to the item. Use :scope for the item itself.

attribute.description: Single attribute or text pseudo-attribute to read.

attributes.description: Ordered attributes or text pseudo-attribute to try.

tools.name: browser.run_javascript
tools.description: Run schema-declared JavaScript in the authorized page with structured arguments and return the script result. Page-specific actions belong in actions.json, not in the runtime.

source.description: JavaScript function body to execute. It receives `args` and `helpers` bindings and may return a structured value.

args.description: Structured arguments passed to the JavaScript body.

tools.name: dom.list_sections
tools.description: List rendered page sections with inferred headings, document offsets, viewport visibility, and optional item counts. Use this before scrolling to rows whose vertical position varies between page loads.

selector.description: CSS selector for section-like containers. Defaults to section and role=region containers.

heading_selector.description: Optional CSS selector used inside each section to identify the section heading.

item_selector.description: Optional CSS selector counted inside each section, useful for carousel rows.

text_contains.description: Optional case-insensitive filter applied to heading and section text.

tools.name: debug.run_javascript
tools.description: Authoring-only fallback that evaluates arbitrary JavaScript through the extension background debugger transport. Use this to prove a page operation before encoding it as portable actions.json behavior; do not treat it as a portable primitive.

source.description: JavaScript function body to evaluate in the inspected page. It receives `args` and `helpers` bindings.

args.description: Structured arguments passed to the JavaScript body.

tools.name: locator.element_info
tools.description: Resolve a visible locator to viewport geometry and a recommended clickable center. When multiple visible elements match, the result includes ambiguous=true, candidate_count, and candidates with text, bounding boxes, and clickable centers; inspect candidates before clicking.

tools.name: viewport.scroll
tools.description: Scroll the current viewport, or a scoped scrollable page region such as a horizontal carousel, by a visible delta.

scope.description: Optional page region used to find a scrollable element instead of scrolling the global viewport.

item_selector.description: Optional item selector used with nearest_ancestor_containing_items to locate the carousel root.

tools.name: pointer.click
tools.description: Move the visible pointer affordance to viewport coordinates and perform a click.

tools.name: pointer.move
tools.description: Move the visible pointer affordance to viewport coordinates without clicking. Use this to show the user what element is being targeted before a follow-up action.

tools.name: pointer.double_click
tools.description: Move the visible pointer affordance to viewport coordinates and perform a double click.

tools.name: pointer.drag
tools.description: Move visibly from one viewport point or locator to another while dragging. Call a site-specific read action after this primitive to verify that the page state actually changed; some applications ignore synthetic drag events.

tools.name: text.insert
tools.description: Insert text into the focused editable target or a requested editable locator. Use a site-specific locator and pointer.click first when the target is not already focused.

target.description: Optional editable target locator. Omit to use the focused editable element.

tools.name: keyboard.press
tools.description: Dispatch a keyboard key or modifier chord to the focused page element. Use this for Enter/Escape/Tab flows after focusing a visible target.

key.description: Key name, or a compact chord such as Meta+A, Control+A, or Shift+Enter.

tools.name: transfer.insert
tools.description: Render a stored transfer item and insert it into a target editable element without resending the full payload through the model.

tools.name: storage.list
tools.description: List the currently cached actions.json.storage bundle paths from extension local storage.

tools.name: task.add
tools.description: Add one or more tasks to the in-session task queue. Pass a single task as text, or many as a tasks array of strings. Use this to externalize a multi-step plan so you do not have to hold the whole loop in memory: enqueue every step first, then pull them one at a time with task.next.

tasks.description: Multiple task descriptions to enqueue in order.

tools.name: task.next
tools.description: Pull the next pending task from the queue and mark it in progress. Returns {done:false, task, remaining} while work remains, or {done:true, remaining:0, ...summary} when the queue is empty (including a summary of every completed and failed task to ground your final report). After finishing a task, call task.complete, then task.next again.

tools.name: task.complete
tools.description: Report the in-progress task as finished. Optionally pass id to target a specific task, status "failed" if it could not be done, and result with a short outcome note. Then call task.next for the following task.

id.description: Task id to complete; defaults to the in-progress task.

tools.name: task.list
tools.description: Return the full task queue with each task id, text, status, and result, plus counts of completed/failed/pending. Use to review progress without changing state.

tools.name: task.clear
tools.description: Remove all tasks from the queue and reset it. Use before seeding a fresh plan.

signals.name: overlay.closed
signals.description: Emitted when the overlay is closed by the user.

signals.name: overlay.minimized
signals.description: Emitted when the overlay is collapsed to its title bar.

signals.name: overlay.expanded
signals.description: Emitted when the overlay is expanded from its minimized title-bar state.

signals.name: overlay.launcher_opened
signals.description: Emitted when the user opens a registered overlay from a visible-page launcher button.
